title: KarpathyVerificationSchema
description: Upfront quality gates mandated before any execution phase begins. Every task card must satisfy this schema before a Builder-Agent begins writing code.

target_goal.description: The true decision, business conclusion, or architectural outcome this work delivers. Must be stated in user-outcome terms, not technical implementation terms.

measurable_criteria.description: Precise, non-vague rubrics determining what a successful result looks like. Each criterion must be independently verifiable. Avoid criteria like 'works correctly' — prefer 'all unit tests pass with 0 failures'.

validation_methods.description: Declared methods used to verify the output. Must name at least one automated validation method.

failure_modes.description: Optional. Explicit descriptions of what a broken or incomplete result would look like. Enumerating failure modes surfaces blind spots before execution begins.

pre_execution_checklist.description: Optional pre-execution checklist confirming the agent has completed upfront verification steps.

spec_reviewed.description: Spec interview completed and scope is fully understood.

decision_checkpoints_confirmed.description: All guarded architectural decisions have explicit operator approval.

protected_files_checked.description: safeguards/protected-files.md has been reviewed against the target file list.

pre_tool_hook_dry_run.description: scripts/pre-tool-hook.ps1 dry run passed for all target files.

examples.target_goal: Add user authentication to the API so that only registered users can access protected endpoints.
measurable_criteria: All protected endpoints return HTTP 401 for unauthenticated requests
All unit tests pass with 0 failures
JWT token expiry is enforced at 15 minutes
Login endpoint returns a token within 200ms at p95
failure_modes: Protected endpoints return 200 for unauthenticated requests
Token validation is skipped in certain code paths
CI pipeline fails due to missing environment variables
